title: StringValidator
$comment:
This is a type used in Stencila Schema for validating [`String`](./string.md)
nodes.

It adapts common JSON Schema string constraints to Stencila's validator
system, allowing length limits and pattern checks to be expressed as
structured validator nodes.

Key properties include `minLength`, `maxLength`, and `pattern`.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

minLength.description: The minimum length for a string node.
minLength.$comment: A string is valid if its length is greater than, or exactly equal to, `minLength`.

maxLength.description: The maximum length for a string node.
maxLength.$comment: A string is valid if its length is less than, or exactly equal to, `maxLength`.

pattern.description: A regular expression that a string node must match.
pattern.$comment: A string is valid if it is matched by the regular expression.
